skills.name: prostate_mpmri_core
skills.description: Core prostate mpMRI workflow: ingest, identify, register, segment, features, report.
checks: Use T2w as fixed reference; register ADC/DWI into T2w space.
Prefer NIfTI outputs from identify_sequences over raw DICOM dirs.
If DWI lacks b-values, treat it as TRACEW and still register.
preconditions: Prostate mask available before feature extraction

skills.name: prostate_lesion_candidates
skills.description: Lesion candidate detection after features and registration (run when high-b DWI is available).
checks: Require whole-gland mask and registered ADC + high-b DWI.
If DWI/ADC QC overlays suggest severe distortion, run correct_prostate_distortion before detect_lesion_candidates.
If high-b DWI is present, run lesion detection before reporting; only skip if high-b DWI is missing.

skills.name: prostate_report_writer
skills.description: Write a prostate MRI report by executing the full mpMRI pipeline: ingest DICOM, identify sequences, register ADC/DWI to T2w, segment prostate, extract ROI features, package evidence, then generate the report.
checks: Generate whole-gland ROI features before any lesion-only feature runs.
Package evidence before generate_report so overlays are available.

PI-RADS v2.1 scoring: PZ uses DWI/ADC (1 normal, 2 diffuse abnormal without focal lesion, 3 focal abnormal without typical T2, 4 marked diffusion <1.5 cm, 5 same >=1.5 cm/EPE); TZ uses T2 (1 typical BPH, 2 encapsulated nodule, 3 atypical, 4 ill-defined low T2 <1.5 cm, 5 same >=1.5 cm).
preconditions: ADC and DWI are registered into T2w space
Whole-gland or zonal mask exists before feature extraction
ROI features are extracted before report generation

skills.name: brain_tumor_core
skills.description: Standard Brain Tumor (BraTS) workflow: Identify sequences, Register structural/functional to T1c, Segment tumor subregions, Extract features, and Report.
checks: Register T2/FLAIR to T1c (or T1 fallback) before segmentation.
Ensure 4-channel aligned inputs before brats_mri_segmentation.
preconditions: T1c (or T1) is the fixed reference
T2 and FLAIR are registered to T1c

skills.name: brain_report_writer
skills.description: Generate a Neuro-Oncology report by executing the full pipeline: Identify T1/T1c/T2/FLAIR, Register to T1c, Segment tumor (WT/TC/ET), and compile evidence.
checks: Use brats_mri_segmentation masks (WT/TC/ET) for ROI features.
Package evidence before generate_report for VLM review.

skills.name: brain_sequence_interpretation
skills.description: Brain MRI qualitative interpretation cheat-sheet for tumor assessment. Use in the Findings section alongside quantitative features.
checks: Use indeterminate language if enhancement/necrosis/edema are not clearly supported.
Cite image tiles or overlays when using VLM-backed qualitative statements.
preconditions: VLM or reviewer has access to image tiles

T1.normal: White matter bright, gray matter darker, CSF black.
T1.tumor: Hypointense mass compared to normal brain.

T1c.normal: Normal brain parenchyma does not enhance.

phrases: Heterogeneous T2 hyperintensity consistent with tumor extent.
Extensive peritumoral FLAIR hyperintensity consistent with edema.

skills.name: cardiac_cine_core
skills.description: Cardiac cine disease workflow: identify cine sequence, run nnUNet segmentation, classify disease group, package evidence, and optionally generate report.
checks: Prefer NIfTI cine input for segment_cardiac_cine (from identify_sequences mapping CINE).
Use local cmr_reverse Task900_ACDC_Phys weights through segment_cardiac_cine.
ACDC ambiguity rule: HCM requires LVEF > 55%; if LVEF < 40% with local thickness increase, classify as MINF.
ACDC ambiguity rule: high LV diastolic volume + low LVEF + only several abnormal contraction segments -> MINF.
ACDC ambiguity rule: dilated LV and RV (with or without RV dysfunction) -> DCM.
ACDC ambiguity rule: RV EF between 40% and 45% is borderline; do not force RV class.
preconditions: CINE input exists and is readable
